text:
Once upon an autumn in a small town, young Lily was obsessed with fairies. Every afternoon she would sit by a cherry tree counting them. She never saw any.

One day, while mumbling her fairy-counting habit out loud, Lily heard rustling leaves. She peeked carefully, and there were birds! Birds making a circle before descending. She was captivated. 

They were beautiful! She never would have thought she'd get to see birds like those. She named them Glitterfeathers. She spent the whole afternoon wandering her backyard, counting their glittery wings.

Eventually, she decided to try to communicate. She'd sing folk songs and count off lines. The birds got used to her. They would join her on these outings. She taught them how to sing her favourite fairy song.

Every weekend Lily and her avian friends would visit a nearby clearing. There, she'd weave nets from flowers and teach the birds to guard her berries. They lived happily ever after,
